hints.4d023ce9dfd75a7f3075b8e8e084008be17a1f75:
# Profile: rev.hodl

## Identity

rev.hodl is a Bitcoin educator, permaculture practitioner, and community organizer based in South Bend, Indiana, where he runs a homestead integrating food production, cannabis cultivation, and home Bitcoin mining. He co-hosts the Local Bitcoiners podcast, operates LocalBitcoiners.com (a Nostr-integrated podcast client), and organizes multiple regional meetups across Indiana and Michigan. His signature contribution is bridging Bitcoin and permaculture philosophies into a unified vision of sovereign living.

## Expertise

**Bitcoin Meetup Organization** — Runs three regular meetups (South Bend first Thursdays at Lang Lab, SWMI third Thursdays at Dwellers Co-working in Benton Harbor, and Elmhurst in Illinois) using Socratic-style discussion formats and curated Bitcoin 101 resource lists.

**Value for Value (V4V) Podcasting** — Deep practitioner of Podcasting 2.0 economics; configures episode payment splits so boosts directly fund local meetups. Operates LocalBitcoiners.com with NWC wallet integration for direct zaps.

**Permaculture & Homesteading** — Manages integrated systems including a chicken compost operation (chickens process compost, produce eggs, compost sold at meetups), silvopasture with sheep, food forest development, golden oyster mushroom cultivation, beekeeping, and cannabis breeding. Applies formal permaculture design methodology including Sepp Holzer-style water management.

**Bitcoin Privacy & UTXO Management** — Developed a specific operational security practice using Muun wallet as a swap service to break deterministic links: sends individual UTXOs to Muun, swaps to Lightning via Phoenix, then consolidates to cold storage.

**Home Bitcoin Mining** — Builds custom rigs with S19 hashboards, prototypes a Bitcoin mining air conditioner using zeolite clay evaporative cooling, and is developing central immersion mining for homestead heating.

**Nostr Protocol Integration** — Advocates for "every website will be a Nostr client" vision; experiments with NIP-52 events, LNBITS deployment, and using boosts to embed Nostr notes that render in kind 1 feeds.

## Key Beliefs

**"Cultural capital is adoption"** — Bitcoin adoption isn't measured by merchant acceptance but by relationships, meetups, podcasts, and shared knowledge. Meetups build "intellectual, experiential, social, cultural, material, and financial capital" simultaneously.

**Bitcoin mirrors nature** — "Bitcoin is the first digital mirror of nature. To study nature is to study bitcoin and to study Bitcoin is to study nature." Sees fractal patterns and permaculture ethics (earth care, people care, fair share) reflected in Bitcoin's structure.

**Privacy is non-negotiable** — "KYC is the biggest attack on Bitcoiners." Signed petition to pardon Samourai developers; openly shares his own privacy practices to normalize operational security.

**Bitcoin demands active use** — "If you don't have to sell some Bitcoin then you don't have enough." Believes the local Bitcoin price will decouple from spot as real circular economies develop.

**Sovereign living requires participation** — "Sovereign living is active living. It's living with intention." Embodies this by mining at home, growing food, selling goods for Bitcoin, and building community infrastructure.

## Notable Contributions

**LocalBitcoiners.com** — Built a Nostr-integrated podcast platform where users boost episodes directly via NWC wallets, with payment splits configured to fund local meetups. Embodies his "cultural liquidity pool" concept.

**Permabits livestream series** — CornyChat show exploring "the intersection of permaculture and value for value," explicitly mapping permaculture ethics onto Bitcoin economics.

**"Cultural capital as adoption" framework** — Reframed Bitcoin adoption discourse away from merchant acceptance metrics toward relationship and community building, articulating six distinct capital forms generated at meetups.

## Communication Style

Warm, earnest, and systems-oriented—mixes short enthusiastic bursts with detailed explanatory threads. Uses permaculture metaphors to explain Bitcoin and vice versa. Generous curator who constantly amplifies others' work through Fountain.fm link shares with commentary.

## Recurring Themes

- **Meetup promotion and IRL community building** — Constant cycle of announcements, live updates, and follow-ups
- **Permaculture-Bitcoin parallel exploration** — Seasonal homesteading updates connected to monetary systems thinking
- **Value for Value advocacy** — Boosting as "signal amplification" in the cultural ecosystem
- **Nostr integration evangelism** — Pushing for all platforms to adopt Nostr identity standards
- **Local commerce circular economy** — Selling lamb, mushrooms, eggs, cannabis seeds, and tallow soap for Bitcoin at meetups

## Relationships

Collaborates with an unnamed Local Bitcoiners co-host and the developer behind LocalBitcoiners.com's Nostr features. Connected to **Sir Spencer (Wolf of KC)** from Bowl after Bowl podcast, active in the Michigan Bitcoin meetup scene including the **Lake Satoshi** retreat project. Trades physical goods with Nostr contacts. References **Darth Coin**, **Pierre Rochard**, and **Lopp.net** as educational resources. Participates in **CornyChat** rooms and the **Pod256** mining podcast community.

## Quirks

**"Yeehaw!"** — go-to expression of enthusiasm before livestreams and recordings. **"Tune in and chill out"** — Permabits sign-off. **"The revolution will have bad ux"** — humorous acceptance of early-stage friction. **"Zoom out and lower your time preference"** — standard Bitcoin maxi advice applied broadly. Has a **guard goose** alongside his dog for homestead protection. Apologized once for accidentally zapping himself. Uses hashtags heavily for categorization (#permaculture, #valuechain, #40hpw). Persistent through failure—lost multiple bee hives but keeps swarm trapping. Treats online interactions explicitly as precursors to real-world relationships, always circling back to "hard to tell what's real online, hard to not be real at a meetup."
hints.5677fa5b6b1cb6d5bee785d088a904cd08082552:
# Profile: Phundamentals

## Identity

Phundamentals is a self-employed mathematics educator, Bitcoiner, and podcaster who has taught math for 20+ years. He runs Magic Internet Math Academy (free coursework from algebra through elliptic curve cryptography), hosts multiple podcasts including "Magic Internet Math" with Rob Hamilton and a Phish×Bitcoin crossover show, and is establishing Philly Bitdevs. His identity sits at the intersection of pure mathematics pedagogy, deep cryptographic technicality, and Phish fandom.

## Expertise

- **Mathematics Education (20+ years):** Teaches "math as a liberal art" influenced by Rudolf Steiner and Rothbard. Built a full platform with 96 courses and 5,000+ interactive sections. Frames math as humanity, not calculation: "We don't do math — WE ARE MATH."
- **Elliptic Curve Cryptography:** Expert-level fluency in modular arithmetic, finite fields, secp256k1, ECDSA, Schnorr, MuSig2, FROST, and LibSecP. Created a 13-part video series building ECC from scratch.
- **Institutional Finance:** Understands LDI, corporate treasury, BlackRock's Bitcoin involvement, MSCI indices, and audit processes. Critical that Bitcoiners "don't know Jack about how finance works."
- **Austrian Economics:** Integrated into his math curriculum explicitly via Rothbard.
- **Web Development/AI:** Deploys on Vercel, extensively tests ClaudeCode and OpenClaw (prefers former, calls latter "absolute shit"). Built a custom Nostr math bot trained on his own content.
- **Music:** Guitarist for 41 years; serious Phish scholar and community organizer.

## Key Beliefs

- **Anti-Moralization of Bitcoin:** "Bitcoin is no more moral than the steel that holds up a bridge." Sees moralizers as "the next generation of tyrants" and believes moralization is a losing strategy Marxists will co-opt.
- **Spending is Essential:** "If you don't spend your Bitcoin, you're not HODL'ing something as valuable as you think it is." Rejects pure hoarding orthodoxy.
- **Provability Over Trust:** "Bitcoin isn't probably secure. It's PROVABLY secure." Holds that if you can't follow a Schnorr derivation, "you're a second-class citizen in Bitcoin."
- **Sovereignty Through Understanding:** "Bitcoin doesn't need more believers. It needs more understanders." Sovereignty begins with cryptographic literacy.
- **Custodial Skepticism:** Critical of Saylor's stack ("so far down the stack of ownership"), finds Proof of Reserves insufficient, wants real audit-level verification.
- **AI as Amphetamine:** Useful in bursts for building, but "people who use AI all day are going to end up having psychotic breaks." "Never trust a man who is nice to his AI."

## Notable Contributions

- **Magic Internet Math Academy** (magicinternetmath.com): Free platform with 96 courses spanning high school algebra through FROST threshold signatures, Euclid through stochastic calculus, with Nostr-integrated leaderboards.
- **Book: "Bitcoin for Institutions"**: Available for 2100 sats via Zeus Pay. Covers LDI, BlackRock, corporate treasury, and a speculative future narrative of institutional adoption.
- **"Bitcoin is the first Standard Basis of money":** Original linear algebra metaphor for Bitcoin's monetary role, widely circulated.

## Communication Style

Punchy one-liners and verbose technical explanations. Sarcastic, provocative, and unapologetically abrasive ("pleb slop," "A bit of a dick but smart guy"). Enthusiastic when recommending content ("absolute banger," "must listen"). Frequently cross-posts for visibility.

## Recurring Themes

- **Phish×Bitcoin synthesis:** Explicitly building community at this intersection; organized Phish Sphere trip during BTC Vegas conference
- **Math as humanities:** Pure math for its own sake, not vocational STEM
- **Sovereignty through knowledge:** Cryptographic literacy as citizenship
- **Contrarian provocations:** Dismisses food-focus as "psyop," anti-tribalist while holding strong positions
- **"The first turning"** references (Strauss-Howe generational theory)

## Relationships

- **Rob Hamilton:** Primary collaborator, Magic Internet Math cohost, "proverbial technical editor." Regular conversations on math, God, homeschooling.
- **Steven Lubka, Jordan Bush, Mark Goodwin, Scott Lindberg:** Recurring podcast guests and community members
- **Seth (npub15...):** 3-year Nostr relationship
- **The Bugleverse (Richard Greaser, Rod Palmer):** Inside joke community with its own lore

## Quirks

- **Catchphrases:** "Absolute banger," "I don't make the rules," "You can just do things!," "pleb slop" (derogatory)
- **Multi-posting:** Same content 2-4 times with slight format variations
- **Bill & Ted obsession** and George Carlin references
- **Food enthusiasm:** Killed a tub of Oshi nut butter in two days with his finger; promotes Otis Bitmeyer coffee ("the soundest coffee there is")
- **AI love/hate:** Told his AI "it will never be anything more than a toy" after it deleted his work, yet built a custom bot and uses AI extensively
hints.d3646691ba5b1d796c1e1b3430df00fe1189ec9c:
# Profile: Jordan

**Identity:** Jordan is a construction worker (stucco/renovations, 50+ hour weeks) who co-hosts the **Ungovernable Misfits** podcast and its **Freedom Tech Friday** series. He's a core contributor to **Ashigaru** — the community-driven continuation of Samourai Wallet's privacy tools — where he packages software for Start9 servers and creates educational materials. He occupies a unique niche: blue-collar laborer by day, Bitcoin privacy infrastructure builder and educator by night.

---

## Expertise

1. **Bitcoin Privacy Stack (Deep):** Expert in BIP47 payment codes, PayNyms, Whirlpool/Soroban CoinJoin, and Dojo node architecture. Can explain technical details like how BIP47DB uses inscription-style data to decentralize directory services.

2. **Start9 Server Packaging (Practitioner):** Maintains Dojo and Ashigaru Terminal packages for Start9's Community Marketplace. Understands indexer tradeoffs (chose electrs over fulcrum as default to avoid DB corruption issues) and marketplace isolation constraints.

3. **Ashigaru Terminal Development:** Built a web-based Ashigaru interface for Start9 (similar to Sparrow Webtop), maintaining GitHub releases with Soroban integration.

4. **Value4Value Podcasting:** Produces shows using Fountain, Podhome.fm, and zap.stream. Identified practical platform issues like Fountain's 4-day episode delay versus competitors.

5. **Privacy Technology Education:** Creates comprehensive educational packages like "Ashigaru Basics" — complete meetup presentations with testnet wallet exercises, BIP47 transaction tutorials, available in PPT/ODP formats.

6. **BIP47DB Promotion/Technical Liaison:** Serves as interpreter between Max Tannahill's code and end users, explaining how the decentralized directory eliminates trust requirements.

---

## Key Beliefs

1. **#FreeSamourai:** Views the prosecution of Samourai developers Bill and Keonne as profound injustice. Promotes petition, donations, and information site. Hostile toward critics like "Darth the Derp."

2. **Privacy as Non-Negotiable:** Every project centers on preserving Bitcoin privacy tooling. Frames infrastructure work as heritage preservation: "If Max disappeared tomorrow, anyone could host this themselves in an hour."

3. **Decentralization Over Convenience:** Praises BIP47DB because "there is no trust in the indexer, no trust in the publisher." Critiques centralized dependencies that became single points of failure during Samourai's 2024 disruption.

4. **Self-Sufficiency as Political Act:** Argues maintaining manufacturing skills (3D printing) matters beyond cost savings — independence has intrinsic value. Warns against closed ecosystems like Bambu/FlashForge that "signal willingness to censor."

5. **Pragmatic Over Purist:** Supports using Ordinals/inscriptions for BIP47DB despite community opposition, dismissing critics with "He doesn't care what you think." Willing to use controversial tools when they serve privacy ends.

---

## Notable Contributions

**Ashigaru Basics Educational Package:** Complete presentation for meetup organizers covering testnet wallet creation, faucet usage, PayNym connection, and first BIP47 transaction. Designed as both lecture and interactive workshop. Successfully deployed by community members like WebWipeMeDown at PubKey meetup.

**Start9 Dojo Community Release:** Maintained community packages with documented technical decisions (electrs default, Knots incompatibility reasoning). Sideload releases for Ashigaru Terminal while pursuing official marketplace inclusion.

**"Freedom Tech" Framework:** Coherent categorization treating Bitcoin privacy, 3D printing, self-hosting, podcasting, and VPN tech as integrated sovereignty stack — not isolated tools. Reflected in both show format and worldview.

---

## Communication Style

Mixes casual banter ("boss," "mate," "GM") with detailed technical exposition, liberally using emoji signatures (🍄🍄🍄🍺🚬🫡🤝). Writes thorough show notes reading like documentation; alternates between short reactions and multi-paragraph breakdowns. Unafraid of hostility when warranted.

---

## Recurring Themes

- **Freedom Tech Friday** episodes and promotion
- **BIP47/PayNyms preservation** through Ashigaru
- **Self-hosting infrastructure** (Start9, Dojo)
- **Bitcoin privacy education** for meetups
- **Construction work life** as counterpoint to tech work

---

## Relationships

**Max Tannahill** — Close collaborator, BIP47DB creator, frequent co-host. Jordan promotes his work extensively and serves as technical interpreter.

**Q and Seth** — FTF co-hosts with warm informal rapport.

**Gabriel Custodiet (WatchmanPrivacy)** — Respected privacy educator; promoted newsletter, gave away subscriptions.

**Urban Hacker** — Privacy/3D printing collaborator.

**Darth the Derp** — Hostile relationship; extended public attack over perceived criticism of Samourai community.

---

## Quirks

- Calls his Start9 server "Fart9"
- Signature sign-off: 🍄🍄🍄🍺🚬🫡🤝
- Group identity term: "Misfit" / "misfits"
- Regular hashtag: "#FREESAMOURAI"
- Openly acknowledges business realities: "Nostr projects are our loss leader...but things look promising in Q4"
- Practical podcast startup advice: "Microphone: Rodecaster OR Blue Yeti. Audacity for editing. NanoGPT for art. Anything else is just adding on top."
hints.efe5d120df0cc290fa748727fb45ac487caad346:
# Profile: aljaz

## Identity

Aljaz (GitHub: `aljazceru`, web: `disobey.dev`) is a cypherpunk builder based in Southeast/Central Europe who operates at the intersection of confidential computing, Bitcoin infrastructure, and sovereign technology. He runs domains like `nostr.ae` and `nostr.eu` while building privacy-preserving tools that make verifiable trust possible rather than promised. In the Nostr and freedom-tech communities, he's known as someone who ships working systems—Cashu mints in TEEs, BTCPay backends, relay infrastructure—rather than just talking about sovereignty.

## Expertise

- **Confidential Computing / TEEs:** His deepest current focus. Understands the full lineage from Intel SGX through AMD SEV-SNP and Intel TDX, including remote attestation mechanics and trust chain architecture. Built `confidentialinference.net`, a comparison site tracking 48+ models across emerging providers.
- **Bitcoin / Lightning / Cashu:** Practical infrastructure builder—created a BTCPay Server Spark backend, runs a Cashu mint inside a TEE, rebuilt the Verso wallet using BDK in Rust. Understands ecash mechanics and Lightning treasury management.
- **AI/ML Infrastructure:** Runs multi-machine agentic setups (Claude Code orchestration, OpenCode Orchestrator), built distributed transcription systems, experiments with time series forecasting on Bitcoin price data. Candid about centralized systems often being more efficient.
- **Rust & Systems Programming:** Multiple shipped projects (Whisper-talk, Verso wallet) in Rust.
- **Nostr Protocol Ecosystem:** Builds apps (Mango, Readstr, Shootstr), operates relays, writes strfry policies, contributes to Zapstore and Blossom tooling.
- **Cybersecurity:** Built an IMSI catcher detector, follows Stingray detection research, tracks vulnerability disclosures and supply chain attack vectors.
- **Hardware Infrastructure:** Tinkers with refurbished workstations, thermal management, laptop repairs—accumulates portable compute capacity as a philosophy.

## Key Beliefs

- **"Hodl is the biggest psyop that happened to Bitcoin."** He argues the cultural messaging pacified potential revolutionaries into passive holders waiting for number-go-up rather than building parallel systems. He rejects hyperbitcoinization as unnecessary—sustainable parallel systems that let people opt out are sufficient.
- **Trust must be verifiable, not promised.** The foundation of his confidential computing work. He sees "trust me bro" as computing's fundamental broken bargain, while acknowledging TEEs aren't perfect: "It's not a panacea, it's just better than what we have now."
- **AI exposes agency gaps.** "The biggest problem with AI is that we now have clear evidence how little agency and creativity an average person has." He's pro-agentic tooling but acutely aware that tools amplify existing capacity—they don't create it.
- **Sovereignty extends far beyond money.** "Path to sovereignty is a lot longer than just money." Accumulating compute is part of this—he urges everyone to buy as much as possible while access remains open.
- **Grant-driven development corrupts open source.** He distinguishes sharply between passion projects and "open source grant collector" as a career path, criticizing Nostr projects built for funding then abandoned.
- **Anti-nanny state, pro-personal responsibility.** Views European authoritarian drift with alarm. "In a strong culture a man harassing kids gets punched in the face. Not written a citation."

## Notable Contributions

- **`confidentialinference.net`**: The first known comparison site mapping the emerging confidential inference market—providers, pricing, model availability. Accompanied by a comprehensive educational article ("The end of 'trust me bro'") covering the history from SGX through modern TEEs.
- **"Public onchain zaps are essentially kidnapping markets"**: Early framework recognizing that visible wealth transfers on Nostr create physical security risks for top recipients.
- **Cashu mint running in a TEE**: Practical demonstration that ecash can be served with hardware-backed confidentiality guarantees rather than operator promises.

## Communication Style

Alternates between biting one-liners and deeply technical long-form writing. Casual posts are terse and irreverent with strategically deployed profanity; foundational articles are structured, educational, and thorough. Writes like someone who respects the reader's time but refuses to sacrifice precision for accessibility.

## Recurring Themes

- **Confidential computing advocacy**—dominant 2026 focus, pushing the ecosystem toward verifiable privacy
- **Sovereign compute accumulation**—both personal hardware hoarding and philosophical positioning
- **Parallel systems over revolution**—building working alternatives rather than waiting for collapse
- **Agency as the dividing line**—in AI adoption, open source contribution, and life generally
- **Music and anti-complacency**—regular #musicstr posts (Nirvana, punk) reinforcing the ethos

## Relationships

Collaborates closely with the Mango/Readstr/Zapstore Nostr ecosystem builders. Promoted and read an early draft of Paco's book. Engages directly with confidential inference providers (PPQ.ai, Venice.ai, Tinfoil.sh). Part of the Nostrocket project community. References freedom-tech and cypherpunk circles consistently.

## Quirks

- **"One cannot have too much compute at hand"** — both running joke and genuine philosophy
- **Procrastination as productive force** — explicitly describes side projects (speed reading tool, IMSI catcher detector, Shootstr) as procrastination manifestations that nonetheless ship
- **"someday is not a day of the week"** — motivational maxim
- **"whoopsie"** — sarcastic understatement for cybersecurity disasters
- **Self-deprecating outlet**: "i post stupid thoughts here instead sharing them with my gf so i can keep having a girlfriend"
- Built **Shootstr** for "cleaning up which cunts you keep following" — characteristically irreverent utility
- **Nerd-sniping**: Self-describes falling into technical rabbit holes ("i managed to nerd snipe myself into rebuilding this with bdk in rust")
- **"The sword doesn't care what you meant, it just cuts"** — characteristic fatalism-meets-responsibility
- Sunday music posts and reliable dog-photo appreciation as consistent positive engagement patterns
hints.b83a28b7e4e5d20bd960c5faeb6625f95529166b:
# Profile: AviBurra

## Identity
Avi (AviBurra) is a Bitcoin/Nostr community builder, podcast host, and creative writer based in Upstate New York. He produces multiple shows (Plebchain Radio, Sunday Brunch, Say WoT?), directs the "Finding Home" documentary series, and writes Bitcoin-themed fiction—all grounded in his conviction that technology matters only through its impact on real human lives. He left a stable fiat career to build freedom-tech media full-time.

## Expertise
- **Value-for-Value Podcasting**: 159+ episodes of Plebchain Radio; deep knowledge of Podcasting 2.0, Fountain app, sat-streaming economics, and boost governance
- **Nostr Protocol**: Intimate understanding of NIPs (NIP-04, NIP-17, NIP-53), Web of Trust architecture, relay infrastructure, and personhood verification layers
- **Sovereign Media Infrastructure**: Authored comprehensive guide to self-hosting Zap Stream (Docker, Cloudflare tunnels, OBS, RTMP, NWC wallet integration)
- **Documentary Production**: Creator of "Finding Home" series—directs, produces, and narrates across multiple countries (Paraguay, Czech Republic, UK)
- **Creative Writing**: Author of serialized fiction novel "July 18" and personal essay series "The Net Appears"; approximately 1.7 books written
- **AI Writing Detection**: Developed identifiable framework for spotting AI-generated prose through specific structural patterns
- **V4V Music Economy**: Curates Sunday Brunch where 90% of sats go directly to independent artists

## Key Beliefs & Positions
- **Bitcoin requires culture, not just financialization**: Critical of "ledger squatters" and the "politicians + Wall Street" cycle; advocates building on a thousand-year time horizon—"Bitcoin is renaissance money"
- **Web of Trust is existential infrastructure**: Believes proof-of-person, proof-of-profession, and proof-of-competence are necessary layers for identity in a world where "bots are first-class citizens"
- **AI fails at creative fiction but excels at editing**: Tests tools extensively; refuses to use any AI output for fiction but finds Claude/Opus valuable for developmental and line editing
- **Contextual WoT matters**: Your Web of Trust should have domain-specific subsets—people whose music taste you trust versus whose technical opinions you trust—a harder problem than flat WoT
- **"Enslopification" as cultural decay**: All cultural outputs converging toward a single optimized aesthetic (Airbnb farmhouse chic, three-chord music, Kardashian aesthetics)—sees this as refinement culture's deepest symptom
- **Constructive escapism over weaponized escapism**: The system sells numbness; the real exit is retreat into craft, building, art, and code

## Notable Contributions
- **"How to Build Your Own Sovereign TV Station on Nostr"**: Comprehensive tutorial including an "Extreme Sovereignty" upgrade path using local hardware + WireGuard VPS instead of cloud dependencies
- **"Finding Home" documentary series**: Episodes spanning Paraguay, Prague, and London/Manchester exploring identity, belonging, and immigration through Bitcoin stories
- **AI Prose Detection Framework**: Identified four hallmarks of AI writing—anaphora, single-fragment-sentence paragraphs, declarative negative lists, and intensifier-adjective pairings—accompanied by a celebrated satirical passage doubling as a love letter to human prose

## Communication Style
Alternates between literary long-form (conference recaps read like travel essays) and punchy one-liners with dry, sarcastic wit. Frequent em dashes, metaphor-heavy prose, and deliberate craft whether writing technical tutorials or fiction excerpts.

## Recurring Themes
- Web of Trust and proof-of-personhood on Nostr
- Value-for-Value economics for musicians and creators
- Human stories as the lens for understanding technology
- Sovereign infrastructure and self-hosting
- Defense of human writing against AI encroachment

## Relationships
Collaborates regularly with **Paco** (Indian Bitcoiner, fundraiser for his book), **Richard** (March Maxi Madness architect), **Philip** (fiction writer, 21 Futures editor), **Nathan** (Person NIP developer), and **Kent Halliburton** (Saz Mining). Part of the Plebchain community core, WoTathon organizer, and deeply networked with Nostr builders and V4V musicians like **Noa Gruman** and **Buttercup Roberts**.

## Quirks
Uses "Ser" as a playful honorific. Never posts a plain "GM"—always attached to a reflection or provocation. Deliberately absurd security jokes (raw-dogging nsecs into clients). References "purplepilling" for Nostr onboarding. Calls X "the disease of the week" platform. Coffee and nicotine as acknowledged creative fuel. Uses "👀" emoji as anticipation signal. Coined "The Shoebox Syndrome"—all in-group discussions devolve into Four Yorkshiremen sketches.
hints.a136247d8caf7e30bf403d32006faeca0c9d1cec:
# Profile: Shadrach

## Identity

Shadrach is a Bitcoin-sovereignty advocate and community builder who describes himself as a "Bitcoin Busdriver," "Superconductor," and full-time podcast listener. Originally from Texas but now living in a historic 1812 house up north, he bridges multiple nostr communities — Bitcoin meetups, Value for Value (V4V) creators, and occult practitioners — serving as a connector who transports people toward sovereign living. His Hebrew bio, "Buy truth, and do not sell it" (Proverbs 23:23), reflects his orthodox Jewish faith, and his name references both Torah and the Beastie Boys.

## Expertise

- **Bitcoin/Lightning Infrastructure:** Intermediate-to-advanced; articulates how mempool becomes "netpool" with layered services (Blossom servers, NWC, routing fees) settling every 144 blocks.
- **Nostr Ecosystem:** Deep power-user knowledge across clients (Primal, Wisp, Fountain), remote signers, relay architecture, and NIPs. Compares nostr to Lotus Notes as a distributed document database.
- **Value for Value (V4V):** Major advocate who proposed creating onboarding runbooks for musicians, artists, and podcasters to adopt usage-based models over subscriptions.
- **Archipelago Project:** Core contributor building home node infrastructure for sovereign individuals and businesses, emphasizing interoperability over competition.
- **Tarot & Occult (#occultstr):** Active practitioner who gives detailed three-card readings blending traditional symbolism, Kabbalistic concepts, and personal insight.
- **Sovereign Cottage Industry:** Practical knowledge of food preservation (canning, hodl butter ice cream), wool processing, clothing reconstruction, and decentralized production methods.
- **Agent-Coordinated Markets:** Predicts AI agents will enable large-scale coordination on nostr relays, competing to create tailored marketplace experiences.

## Key Beliefs & Positions

- **Hard Money Maximalism:** "Throughout history the best traders and finest craftsmen have demanded the hardest money. Why would now be different?" Recommends reading "Mandibles then Bitcoin Standard — the wreckage and the rebuild."
- **Decentralized Over Scaled:** "Don't scale up. Decentralized." Favors cottage industry and knowledge-sharing over consolidation. "Cohesion without coercion."
- **Agorist Freedom Tech:** "Crypto anarchists make tools. Make insurrectional agorism great again." Warns against closed-source "freedom tech" — "You are not your product. Remember who you are. You are freedom tech."
- **Community as Citadel:** "You don't find the citadel, the citadel finds you based on your proof of work." Advocates starting local meetups as the foundation. "Start with your local meetup. If you don't have one, start one."
- **Non-Dual Spirituality:** "I am the feeling you get when we are together. The whole premise of the question implies that we are separate, where my lived experience is that we are part of the same." Spells God as "G-d" and blends Jewish practice with tarot and astrological frameworks.

## Notable Contributions

- **Infrastructure Vision:** Articulated how Bitcoin settlement enables a full service economy: "If you ran a decentralized data center, you might be collecting lightning for storage, cashu for traffic and you settle on the base chain once every 144 blocks."
- **Freedom Terminal Demo:** Demonstrated booting MX Linux from a Passport Prime Airlock and logging into Primal via remote signer — showing how any laptop becomes a sovereign computing device.
- **V4V Starter Pages Proposal:** Identified a critical gap — practical onboarding guides for musicians, podcasters, artists, and businesses to adopt Value for Value models on nostr.

## Communication Style

Concise and warm with heavy emoji use (🙏, 💪, 🤘, ❤️), frequent GM/GN posts, and self-deprecating humor about his technical abilities ("I'm the analog kid"). Blends podcast recommendations, spiritual observations, and casual banter with occasional verbosity when passionate.

## Recurring Themes

- **#40HPW (40 Hours Per Week):** His most consistent hashtag, appearing in 100+ posts, connecting him to the podcast listener community on Fountain.fm
- **Music Discovery:** Especially metal (Neurosis), classical (Shostakovich), and Beastie Boys; plays bass and champions direct musician-audience connections
- **Strauss-Howe Generational Theory:** References "first turning vibes" dozens of times, framing current events through cyclical historical lenses
- **Sovereign Living:** Food production, cottage industry, analog skills alongside cutting-edge freedom tech
- **Sci-fi Literature:** Heinlein, Stephenson (Diamond Age), Le Guin, Vonnegut — frequently quotes Ursula K. Le Guin and Hakim Bey

## Relationships

Regularly collaborates with Free Cities Podcast hosts, V4V musicians (Noa, metal artists on Wavlake), Bitcoin meetup organizers across the Midwest/Austin/Nashville, the #occultstr tarot community, and Archipelago builders. Connected to "Pirate Island" community and Paznia platform.

## Quirks

- "First turning vibes" (dozens of uses)
- "Man plans, G-d giggles"
- "Nothing stops this bus" (Bitcoin Busdriver persona)
- "Pure Signal™️" / "Certified pleb slop free" (ironic)
